other_observers: Cole Wild and Nathan Pieplow; first spotted by me
description: the bird was about the size of a Horned Lark, but with a somewhat different shape - the tail was shorter and wider, and the bird overall seemed a bit chunkier. The bill was thin and fairly short. I could not see the legs.  The underparts of the bird were white with a slightly off-white tinge. There was a necklace of black streaks across the chest, fairly bold and easy to see. The upperparts of the bird were on the light end of medium brown. The tail was black with two white outer tail feathers.Behaviors: The bird was only observed flying overhead, though it was close, in excellent light, and provided a fairly long and excellent look.Call: The bird emitted a Barn Swallow like "squeet" about eight times at it flew overhead, mostly in closely spaced doublets.Plumage: unknown
